---
title: "Methodology 2 — Frank-Wolfe on the polytope"
subtitle: "Linear minimisation oracle, no projections"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
*Draft outline — content to come.*
:::

## The Frank-Wolfe iteration

At iterate $\boldsymbol t^{(r)}$:

1. **Linear minimisation oracle (LMO)**:

$$
\boldsymbol s^{(r)} \;\in\;
\arg\min_{\boldsymbol u \in \mathcal{T}_k}
\langle \boldsymbol g(\boldsymbol t^{(r)}),\,\boldsymbol u\rangle
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
\boldsymbol s^{(r)} = \text{vertex with the $k$ smallest entries of $\boldsymbol g(\boldsymbol t^{(r)})$.}
$$

2. **Step**: $\boldsymbol t^{(r+1)} = (1 - \alpha)\,\boldsymbol t^{(r)} + \alpha\,\boldsymbol s^{(r)}.$

3. **Iterate** until $\boldsymbol t^{(r)}$ converges to a vertex.

## Why no projections

The cardinality polytope is a *combinatorial* set; projecting onto it is itself a hard problem. Frank-Wolfe avoids the projection — every step just requires the LMO, which is a sort.

## Vertex-approach guarantee

$$
\|\boldsymbol t^{(r)} - \boldsymbol s^*\|_\infty
\;\le\;
(1 - \alpha)^r \,\|\boldsymbol t^{(0)} - \boldsymbol s^*\|_\infty.
$$

Linear convergence to the chosen vertex once the LMO direction settles.

> Placeholder for visual: a small 3D figure of FW iterates marching toward a vertex of the polytope.

---

[← Boolean relaxation](01-relaxation.qmd) · [Next → Homotopy](03-homotopy.qmd)
